name: Palworld
author: dannyhuggins@proton.me
description: Fight, farm, build and work alongside mysterious creatures called Pals in this completely new multiplayer, open world survival and crafting game!
startup: if [ "${AUTO_UPDATE}" = "true" ] || [ "${AUTO_UPDATE}" = "1" ]; then echo "Running auto-update via SteamCMD..." && steamcmd +@sSteamCmdForcePlatformType linux +force_install_dir /home/container +login anonymous +app_update 2394010 validate +quit || true; fi && ./Pal/Binaries/Linux/PalServer-Linux-Shipping Pal -port={{SERVER_PORT}} -publicport={{SERVER_PORT}} -servername="{{SERVER_NAME}}" -players={{MAX_PLAYERS}} -adminpassword="{{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}" $(if [ -n "$SERVER_PASSWORD" ]; then echo "-serverpassword=\"${SERVER_PASSWORD}\""; fi) -rcon $(if [ "${PUBLIC_LOBBY}" = "true" ]; then echo "-publiclobby"; fi) $(if [ -n "${PUBLIC_IP}" ]; then echo "-publicip=${PUBLIC_IP}"; fi) $(if [ -n "${PUBLIC_PORT}" ]; then echo "-publicport=${PUBLIC_PORT}"; fi) $(if [ "${USE_PERF_THREADS}" = "true" ]; then echo "-useperfthreads -NoAsyncLoadingThread -UseMultithreadForDS"; fi) $(if [ -n "${WORKER_THREADS}" ]; then echo "-NumberOfWorkerThreadsServer=${WORKER_THREADS}"; fi) $(if [ "${REST_API_ENABLED}" = "true" ]; then echo "-restapienabled -restapiport=${REST_API_PORT}"; fi)

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

cd /mnt/server

## download and install steamcmd
STEAMCMD_URL="https://steamcdn-a.akamaihd.net/client/installer/steamcmd_linux.tar.gz"
if [ ! -f "steamcmd/steamcmd.sh" ]; then
    echo "Installing SteamCMD..."
    mkdir -p steamcmd
    curl -sSL "${STEAMCMD_URL}" | tar -xvzf - -C steamcmd/
    chmod +x steamcmd/steamcmd.sh
else
    echo "SteamCMD already installed, skipping download."
fi

export STEAMCMD_DIR="./steamcmd"

## install game
echo "Installing Palworld Dedicated Server..."
./steamcmd/steamcmd.sh \
    +@sSteamCmdForcePlatformType linux \
    +force_install_dir /mnt/server \
    +login anonymous \
    +app_update 2394010 validate \
    +quit || echo "[WARN] SteamCMD install returned exit code $?"

echo "Game installed. Setting permissions..."
chmod -R o+w /mnt/server/steamapps/ || true
chmod +x /mnt/server/PalServer.sh || true

## set up 64 bit libraries
mkdir -p /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64
cp -v steamcmd/linux64/steamclient.so /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so || true

## copy template config file
echo "Copying template config file..."
if [ -f "/mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini" ]; then
    mkdir -p "/mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer"
    cp "/mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini" "/mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini"
    echo "Config file created from template."
else
    echo "No DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini found - server will create one on first start."
fi

echo "Installation complete."

variables.name: Server Name
variables.description: The name of your Palworld server as shown in the server browser

variables.name: Server Description
variables.description: Description of your server shown in the server browser

variables.name: Admin Password
variables.description: Password used to obtain administrative privileges via in-game chat or RCON

variables.name: Server Password
variables.description: Password required for players to join the server. Leave empty for no password.

variables.name: Max Players
variables.description: Maximum number of players who can join the server

variables.name: Auto Update
variables.description: Automatically update the server via SteamCMD on restart

variables.name: Public Lobby
variables.description: Register this server on the community server list

variables.name: Public IP
variables.description: Manually specify the external public IP for community server listing

variables.name: Public Port
variables.description: Manually specify the external public port for community server listing

variables.name: RCON Port
variables.description: Port number used for RCON connections

variables.name: REST API Enabled
variables.description: Enable the REST API for server management

variables.name: REST API Port
variables.description: Listening port for the REST API

variables.name: Worker Threads
variables.description: Number of worker threads for multi-threaded processing. Maximum is CPU thread count minus 1.
